review_text:
Had a great one night stay after picking up our rental. Really friendly and helpful staff, warm welcome. Helped sort out an initial payment issue as well.

Sites are decent size and you have some space between. Basic kitchen and BBQ area which was very, very clean. The amenities were also spotless and the showers were great. Very quiet at night and there are some walking trails in the area.  It's a perfect distance from Melbourne to get you out of the city but still has everything you need to stock up. ould stay ag
Fantastic location and exceptionally clean. Stayed in both, the cabin and with a caravan. Highly recommended.
Clean and quiet.  The gardens and facilities impeccably maintained
A very nice van park very well maintained beautiful gardens lots of care given to surroundings.

This park is so clean and OMG they actually have a sign on amenities saying when they will be cleaned daily...instead of leaving us to guess. And the showers are just like home with a door and a shower hose with nozzle. Park really is a garden due the retirement cottages with their beautiful roses.
Bery generous and friendly staff

We stayed here over this long weekend period, we can not fault this place of accommodation at all. Close to everything the location is perfect, the cabin was well maintained and the outside was just as better, gardens is cared for. The pool, park, BBQ area and laundry are all perfect and well kept. The reception and service was beautiful and welcoming. Thank you for having us.
Pretty good!

An absolute hidden gem in a lovely rural town. The cabin / caravan park would be the best in Victoria , the gardens are absolutely beautiful around the cabins and the caravan sites. The ablutions are modern and spotlessly clean and the bonus of a bath mat was every women’s dream. Pam at the office was welcoming and very helpful. After 6 months on the road and a lot of caravan parks between Cairns and Warragul , Warragul Gardens is certainly No 1 - Congratulations to all and thank you for such a beautiful stay, we have vowed to return and actually stay a few days or a week in the future.
Pretty good

A lovely little holiday spot that offers a variety of accomodation options in a convenient location. It isn't just well maintained.... it has beautifully manicured gardens, clean facilities and nothing broken or out of order. As far as holiday parks go, that is rare!

I enjoyed the quiet and calm environment....for the most part. Admittedly, I didn't quite look at the map close enough to notice a train line nearby but thankfully, it really was only audible to me the first couple of days, and after that it just faded into the background.

The cabins are adorable but it would be important to note that they are not accessible friendly, as they each only have one entry at the top of some steep steps. The bathroom also had a door that could only be opened half way due to the positioning of a protruding towel rail. This was a challenge for me at times even when my mobility is at a near normal level. It would be good to see these towel rails replaced perhaps with shallower individual rails placed at different heights on the wall as opposed to limiting how far a door can be opened.

The housekeepers do a wonderful job, and should be applauded for providing such a high standard of care. I've stayed at many 5-star hotels where less attentive cleaning is provided.

And best of all, it's dog friendly!!
Nice place, a bit pricey for where it is, but is  clean and green. Amenities where clean and modern. Only problem was nothing in the kitchen worked for breakfast,  put a read dampener on my day

Terrible place for an unpowered site. Paid $50 a night. Got put right next to the caravaners toilet dump point (where they dump all their bodily waste) Train line right behind you that interrupts your sleep and flocks of loud birds waking you up at 1, 3, 5am… not the parks fault on that one but it’s a downside. It’s just up there with the worse and probably the most expensive unpowered sites going around on this trip.

Neat as a pin. Staff are easy to deal with, Amenities are prestige and clean all the time, The Gardens are a credit to your staff. 1 of the nicest caravan parks we've stayed in whilst travelling. Thankyou Warragul Gardens
Neat,tidy, welcoming.  Ideal location for town where you will find a great shopping precinct, bowling/croquet club, brilliant sports centre with 3 pools and a hydropool.
A well manicured park nice and quiet.

Stayed on a powered site for 4 nights. Would have to be the most well maintained park we've stayed in. Staff there are really helpful and it's within walking distance to town.
We would definitely stay here again.
Clean room, great service, thank you!

To pay $60 for a caravan site that was so tight and small is ridiculous.  We had the rudest noisiest neighbours who stayed up late socialising that kept us awake ... you know who you are on site 4.  Their dogs did poos in our site and they refused to pick them up.  Never going back there again.

One of the most exspensive on my trip around australia except for some up the east coast .nice park but they charge what they like because no opposition. Showground needs to open for camping then they will reduce there fees
Train is close, very neat and tidy.

Slightly priced higher than others.

Seperate clean bedrooms. Clean place.
Cooking facility. They have a pool
They have coin laundry place.

Place was clean and organised but can seem a bit pricy.

We had a power site, great place clean, staff excellent.
Would highly recommend

A very civilized, well maintained caravan park, with a modest number of tent spaces. Communal bbq area and kitchen with toaster, microwave, hot waterboiler, TV, washing up (double sink) with detergent and spunges. Showers with bathmat, plenty of hooks to hang clothing and towels in both the showers and the toilets. All in good nick and super clean. Showers and toilets wheelchair accessible. Communal kitchen and BBQ are at standing height, so not wheelchair accessible. Playground for kids. Wifi. Walking distance to supermarket, town centre, railway station.
just stopped overnight and the caravan park had concrete slabs and nice grassed area - amenities clean & modern and staff very friendly and professional, no complaints
Great cabin style accommodation. Extremely clean and maintained. Loved our stay and look forward to coming back.

While the cleanliness and aesthetics of the park are nice, I wouldn’t recommend staying here. The owners were rude, or came off that way at least, but I suppose if you can afford to be narcissistic and finicky then it is what it is.
Plenty of space between caravans
Clean,quiet, close to all the town has to offer.
a good van park
Gorgeous little caravan park. Loved it . Meticulously cared for and a close walk to town.
Really nice place- beautiful grounds- couldnt complain

Probably the best Caravan Park where we have stayed. The staff were very friendly and accommodating. Amenities were spotless and modern. The gardens and lawn surroundings are beautifully maintained with obvious love and care. Centrally located. This is a pet friendly Caravan Park which is enhanced with a walking path that takes you past a lovely adjacent park then into the town of Warragul.
Lady on the phone sounded friendly but Apparently "we're very sorry but we dont accommodate for locals of warragul.. I find that absurd.. just my opinion
Great place

Cabin was nice, but, wifi reception and internet capacity was appalling. I'm sure they're still on dial up.
Tv reception even worse, every channel was either pixellating or no signal at all. Management was unable to help, had no idea how to fix and really didn't seem to give two hoots. They got their money, that's all they cared about.

Danke für den Aufenthalt.... Ein wirklich schöner und sehr gepflegter Platz. Wir empfehlen Sie gerne weiter.
Zimmer ist bei uns ein Stellplatz mit Strom, sehr gross..

Great well maintained caravan park.  Has a bike track near and sporting fields and kids play ground all within walking distance.

10 minute walk to CBD.

A picture perfect campground with beautifully maintained gardens and immaculately clean ammenities.
On arrival, we were welcomed by Pam and offered local advice. Plus, reception has an impressive collection of tourist brochures, which is handy as there is no Information Centre in Warragul.
The park offers a substantial choice of books and dvds to borrow from the Laundry ‘library’.
The pool is only small, but didn’t matter on those days when the temperature reached 38 degrees.
A lovely walk or ride to the town centre along the creek and through parkland made it easy to have a wander and contribute to the local economy.
A beer at the ‘ Merch’( The Commercial) and a must do visit to The Moorland Butcher for the best T Bone we’ve had since leaving Imbil, QLD.

This is the best kept caravan park I have been to. Lovely staff. The gardens, camp sites, everything are very well maintained. Close to town, and down the road from a big park and indoor leisure centre. New looking park in the caravan park, and a clean pool. Our cabin is lovely and clean. Highly recommend.

Lovely neat and tidy retirement village with van space in the middle.
Drove out as $55 pn. was to pricey.
Clean, good and not expensive.

So very clean and well maintained the garden settings are beautiful. Not hard to relax here and take it all in.Very impressed with the cleanliness of our cabin. There is a train line along one side of the property but honestly wasn't a bother! Well worth a stay id gladly return again!

What a lovely place to stop! We had an unpowered site and the grass to set up on was lovely, the shared kitchen and eating area is superb and amenities are first class. Would recommend for anyone passing through that wants a breather.
Very well maintained grounds. Interesting that there is retirement village within the park

Absolute gem of a place. We are staying in one of the Cabins, perfect for our family.

Very clean, quiet and nice to wake up in.

Will be returning!
Fantastic stay away- not far from Gumbuya world making it extra fun!
Always a pleasure to stay here. Fantastic amenities.  Wonderful staff

If you want a very clean and quiet caravan and cabin accommodation in Warragul, this place offers everything you need. Also nice managers. We stayed in cabin and it was like home away from home. Highly recommended.

Cannot comment on rooms as we have our own camper. Very happy with our stay here. Beautiful green grass and lovely well maintained gardens. Very nice staff. Also centrally located to everything. Were only staying 2 nights but didn't realise it was a long weekend. We were fortunate to be able to stay and extra 5 nights.
A 5 star caravan park in a lovely setting!
Cleanest facilities and ablution blocks ever! Only stayed overnight, but really impressed.
The staff were very friendly. The cabin was a generous size, and well maintained. Would recommend this park as a great place to stay.
Nice park.  Very helpful staff
A nice, relaxed holiday park to stay at.
We spent 2 nites,10 Years ago.Very very nice place.I could retire there,if I did not live in Canada.

We are waiting for a mechanical upgrade on our tow car, the staff have been so accomodating. Everything is kept so tidy, amenities are very clean, swings and a pool for the kids and adults a public BBQ, dump point, everything you need. Can't say thank you enough.

Stayed in an unpowered site with our 4wd. Lovely caravan park, very green and tidy. Amenities were immaculate! Site was $45 for one night, cheaper than the national park. Staff were also lovely. Will definitely be back.

Had four nights in a deluxe cabin.
Beautiful gardens and quiet park to stay in.
Cabin was excellent, everything you need for a short stay.
Very clean.
Beautiful walks by the creek from the cabins to town.
Lovely staff but the beds are bricks, slept on the couch most the time as that was a little more comfortable
Great places to stay always friendly staff
Jolie, bien fleurie, très propre 👌 personnel au top 👍
Very nice park very clean staff were very friendly.

Warragul Gardens Holiday Park is a hidden gem nestled amongst the rolling hills and charming towns of Victoria's Gippsland region. During my recent stay, I was thoroughly impressed by the park's numerous amenities, impeccable cleanliness, and welcoming atmosphere.

Top-Notch Check-In and Service:

Check-in was a breeze, with the friendly hosts efficiently guiding me through the process and providing me with all the necessary information. They were incredibly helpful and knowledgeable, answering all my questions and offering recommendations for local attractions and activities.

Immaculate Facilities:

The park's facilities were spotless, from the modern camp kitchen and laundry to the well-maintained amenities block. I was particularly impressed by the cleanliness of the showers and toilets, which were always well-stocked and maintained to a high standard.

Spacious and Shaded Camp Sites:

One of the park's most appealing features is its spacious and shaded camp sites. The large areas under towering trees provided ample room for campers to set up their tents, caravans, or motorhomes, offering both privacy and protection from the elements. During my stay, the shade was invaluable as it provided a welcome respite from the summer heat.

Convenient Location:

Warragul Gardens Holiday Park is perfectly situated, just a short walk from the heart of Warragul town. This convenient location allowed me to easily explore the town's shops, cafes, and restaurants without needing to drive.

Overall, Warragul Gardens Holiday Park exceeded my expectations in every way. The combination of smooth check-in, friendly hosts, clean facilities, spacious and shaded camp sites, and a convenient location makes it the perfect choice for a relaxing and enjoyable getaway. I wouldn't hesitate to recommend it to anyone seeking a 5-star camping experience in the heart of Gippsland.
The area nature and best air,quality

Fantastic place to stay in a great location. On arrival the lady at reception was very welcoming and friendly. Stayed in an ensuite cabin.  Accomodation is quiet, clean and comfortable, has everything you may need.

We really enjoyed staying at this park. We loved how clean, organized, green and beautiful it was. It's a short walk to town with many places to eat and shops. The park is also a retirement village so it had an extra soothing vibe and it was amazingly quiet at night. There is a railway right next to the park and a million cockatoos flying in the evening making noises but it didn't bother us at all. Thanks for having us
Best park home we've ever stayed in. Gardens are stunning.
Very well kept and run. Stayed 2 nights in a powered site.
Great holiday park,  awesome staff,  always clean and maintained
Pokey park with vans crammed in. Probably the most disgusting dump point I’ve used. Surely, it can’t be council approved.
We only stayed overnight. Facilities were clean, site level. It’s not far from town
Small caravan bays. Everything else was good
